_meta.source: public websites + G2 + fin.ai/pricing + customer pages, re-verified line by line on 2026-06-12
_meta.warning: This data is curated for demo purposes. G2 counts and pricing move — validate against live competitor sites before quoting.

typewise.name: Typewise
typewise.channels_note: Homepage channel list verbatim — ChatGPT and Claude are listed as channels (agentic-commerce positioning)
typewise.voice_status: listed as a channel on the typewise.app homepage
typewise.data_residency: EU/Switzerland hosting available (security page: EU, Switzerland and/or US depending on client setup)
differentiators: Sits inside your existing helpdesk — no rip-and-replace
AI Assistant for agents + autonomous AI Agents in one orchestration layer
EU/Swiss hosting available — strong story for European data-residency requirements
Multilingual heritage from the keyboard product
typewise.buyer_persona_fit: EU/DACH enterprise (retail, logistics, utilities) that already runs a major helpdesk and wants AI layered on top rather than a platform migration

fin.name: Fin by Intercom
fin.g2_reviews_note: ≈3,800 as of 2026-06 (G2 listing; page title still says '2900+')
fin.voice_status: live (Fin Voice since 2025, Fin Voice 2 shipped 2026)
differentiators: Massive G2 footprint and SERP dominance
Works with the customer's existing helpdesk (fin.ai: 'works seamlessly with any helpdesk') — not Intercom-only
Aggressive SEO on competitor comparison pages
weaknesses_vs_typewise: US data residency by default — EU residency is an option, not the default posture
Per-outcome pricing makes cost scale directly with volume — model the prospect's real resolution mix

decagon.name: Decagon
differentiators: Notion, Duolingo, Hertz, Eventbrite, Bilt as logos
weaknesses_vs_typewise: Black-box criticism circulating in competitor comparison content; Decagon answered publicly with Trace View — expect them to rebut it
US-anchored go-to-market; no public DACH customer stories as of 2026-06 (note: fresh $250M raise earmarked for expansion)

sierra.name: Sierra (Bret Taylor)
founders: Bret Taylor (ex-Salesforce co-CEO, OpenAI board chair)
differentiators: Brand of choice for top-down enterprise sales
weaknesses_vs_typewise: Replacement-narrative scares CS leaders watching headcount cuts
Limited SEO/community presence — relies on top-down
No public DACH customer story as of 2026-06

zendesk_ai.name: Zendesk AI (AI agents / Copilot — formerly Answer Bot)
differentiators: Lowest switching cost for existing Zendesk customers
weaknesses_vs_typewise: Only useful if you're already on Zendesk
Agent quality is debated in the market — but Zendesk acquired Ultimate and is investing heavily, so verify rather than assume
